collisions.paper: Li et al., 2026. Cautious Weight Decay. ICLR 2026. arXiv:2510.12402
collisions.overlap: Uses Lyapunov + LaSalle for convergence of binary WD; sliding-mode analysis on stationary manifold. Our work generalizes to arbitrary continuous time-varying WD schedules.

collisions.paper: Sun et al., 2025. Investigating the Role of Weight Decay in Enhancing Nonconvex SGD. CVPR 2025
collisions.overlap: Provides generalization bound for fixed WD with worst-case alignment delta_T. Our cumulative alignment bound extends their framework to trajectory-level analysis.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2026. Decoupled Orthogonal Dynamics: Regularization for Deep Network Optimizers (AdamO). arXiv:2602.05136
collisions.overlap: Radial/tangential decomposition of WD dynamics; identifies 'Radial Tug-of-War'; architecture-aware rules. Complementary but does not derive convergence certificates or optimal WD schedules.

collisions.paper: Kondo & Iiduka, 2025. Accelerating SGDM via Learning Rate and Batch Size Schedules: A Lyapunov-Based Analysis. arXiv:2508.03105
collisions.overlap: Novel Lyapunov function for SGDM with dynamic LR and batch size schedules. Same Lyapunov methodology but applied to different hyperparameters (not WD).

collisions.paper: Defazio, 2025. Why Gradients Rapidly Increase Near the End of Training. arXiv:2506.02285
collisions.overlap: Shows WD acts as gradient-to-weight ratio controller in normalized layers; proposes AdamC. Empirical analysis without formal convergence certificates.

collisions.paper: Holzl et al., 2025. Gradient-Weight Alignment as a Train-Time Proxy for Generalization. NeurIPS 2025. arXiv:2510.25480
collisions.overlap: Empirical study of gradient-weight alignment dynamics. No formal generalization bound linking alignment to WD.

collisions.paper: AlphaDecay, NeurIPS 2025. Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. arXiv:2506.14562
collisions.overlap: Spectral-aware per-module WD guided by heavy-tail theory. Different principle from alignment-based framework; could be shown as special case within certified band.

collisions.paper: Franke et al., NeurIPS 2024. Improving Deep Learning Optimization through Constrained Parameter Regularization (CPR).
collisions.overlap: Per-parameter constrained regularization via augmented Lagrangian. Different approach (constraint optimization) from Lyapunov control framework.

collisions.paper: Kobayashi et al., NeurIPS 2024. Weight decay induces low-rank attention layers. arXiv:2410.23819
collisions.overlap: L2 regularization on factorized attention = nuclear norm regularization. Architecture-specific WD effect, complementary to our scheduling focus.

collisions.paper: Kosson et al., 2024. Rotational Equilibrium: How Weight Decay Balances Learning Across Neural Networks.
collisions.overlap: WD induces rotational equilibrium balancing learning across layers. Complementary dynamical systems perspective.

collisions.paper: Hassan Saoud, 2025. Composite Lyapunov Criteria for Stability and Convergence. arXiv:2510.08259
collisions.overlap: Provides the composite Lyapunov mathematical tool. Applied to inertial gradient systems, not to WD scheduling.

collisions.paper: Newhouse, 2025. Duality, Weight Decay, and Metrized Deep Learning. MIT MEng Thesis
collisions.overlap: Unified duality-based framework where SGD, AdamW, Muon are special cases. Unifies optimizers, not WD scheduling methods.

collisions.paper: Xie et al., 2023. On the Overlooked Pitfalls of Weight Decay (SWD). NeurIPS 2023
collisions.overlap: Gradient-norm-aware WD scheduling. No unified convergence theory. Shown as special case in our framework.

collisions.paper: E et al., 2017. Maximum Principle Based Algorithms for Deep Learning. JMLR 2017
collisions.overlap: Foundational PMP for deep learning (treats depth as time). Does NOT apply PMP to WD scheduling.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Core novelty is the Lyapunov certificate for general time-varying WD with computable certified band (no prior work). PMP-WD bang-bang optimality is the strongest unique contribution. Formal subsumption of 5+ WD methods under one certificate is genuinely new. Cumulative alignment bound is the weakest claim (incremental extension of Sun et al.). Time sensitivity moderate: 3-4 month window.

collisions.paper: Defazio, 2025. Why Gradients Rapidly Increase Near the End of Training. arXiv:2506.02285
collisions.overlap: Shows that in normalized layers, gradients are orthogonal to weights and WD primarily controls effective LR. This substantially anticipates the 'alignment mirage' thesis.

collisions.paper: D'Angelo et al., NeurIPS 2024. Why Do We Need Weight Decay in Modern Deep Learning?
collisions.overlap: Demonstrates WD's key role under BN is loss stabilization / effective LR control, not regularization in the traditional sense.

collisions.paper: Zhu et al. Three Mechanisms of Weight Decay.
collisions.overlap: Identifies three distinct WD mechanisms depending on architecture/optimizer. BN confound partially characterized.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Core thesis partially anticipated by Defazio (2025) and D'Angelo et al. (NeurIPS 2024). Systematic alignment SNR measurement and random mask control on BN architectures (iter_003 data) are novel contributions but insufficient for standalone paper. Better integrated as ablation within cand_lyapunov_unified.

collisions.paper: Kobayashi et al., NeurIPS 2024. Weight decay induces low-rank attention layers. arXiv:2410.23819
collisions.overlap: Proves L2 on factorized attention matrices = nuclear norm regularization, inducing low-rank. Core theoretical result this candidate builds on.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., Feb 2026. Decoupled Orthogonal Dynamics (AdamO). arXiv:2602.05136
collisions.overlap: Architecture-aware rules for scale-invariant layers; radial-only decay preserves tangential learning.

collisions.paper: AlphaDecay, NeurIPS 2025. Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. arXiv:2506.14562
collisions.overlap: Already implements module-wise adaptive WD based on spectral properties.

collisions.paper: Franke et al., NeurIPS 2024. Constrained Parameter Regularization (CPR).
collisions.overlap: Per-parameter adaptive regularization already differentiates parameter types.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Core claim faces significant prior art from Kobayashi et al. (NeurIPS 2024), AlphaDecay (NeurIPS 2025), AdamO (Feb 2026), and CPR (NeurIPS 2024). The factorial ablation design is methodologically novel but not sufficient for standalone paper. Better as discussion/experiment section in cand_lyapunov_unified.
